Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper explicitly calls ZmBx12 the maize DIMBOA-Glc OMT / DIMBOA-Glc O-methyltransferase.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: ZmBx12 is used to alter benzoxazinoid levels and is discussed within conserved benzoxazinoid biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Results and discussion specifically concern O-methylation of DIMBOA-Glc to HDMBOA-Glc.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: The study states the core benzoxazinoid pathway is conserved and examines DIMBOA-Glc O-methylation as a later regulatory step affecting DIMBOA-Glc/HDMBOA-Glc pools.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: ZmBx12 is introduced as a DIMBOA-Glc OMT and overexpression causes HDMBOA-Glc accumulation at the expense of DIMBOA-Glc/DIMBOA, consistent with methylation of DIMBOA-Glc to HDMBOA-Glc.

Primary_Substrate.reason: ZmBx12 is described as a DIMBOA-Glc OMT, so DIMBOA-Glc is the supported substrate.

Primary_Product.reason: HDMBOA-Glc is the stated product of DIMBOA-Glc O-methylation and accumulates strongly in ZmBx12-overexpressing wheat.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The paper directly links ZmBx12 activity/overexpression to formation and accumulation of HDMBOA-Glc.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The study frames DIMBOA-Glc O-methylation as a metabolic switch/regulatory step between aphid and caterpillar resistance.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: HDMBOA-Glc is the metabolite produced/accumulated downstream of ZmBx12 expression.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: HDMBOA-Glc is one of the benzoxazinoids measured throughout the study.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Results show OeBx12 plants with elevated HDMBOA-Glc are less preferred by caterpillars and have reduced callose inducibility and lower aphid resistance due to DIMBOA-Glc depletion.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Figures 1B and 1C show elevated HDMBOA-Glc in leaves and phloem sap of OeBx12 plants.

Applied_Species.reason: ZmBx12 was expressed in transgenic wheat.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The plant material section identifies wheat as Triticum aestivum.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The wheat line used was Bobwhite SH 98 26.

Source_Species.reason: ZmBx12 is explicitly described as from maize.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The gene prefix Zm and text identify maize as Zea mays.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Methods state the complete open reading frame of ZmBx12-CML322 was amplified, supporting CML322 as source variety.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The transgene was placed under the constitutive maize UBIQUITIN1 promoter / ZmUbi1 promoter.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Results state two T2 homozygous transgenic lines constitutively expressed ZmBx12.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: ZmBx12 is explicitly an O-methyltransferase and discussed among benzoxazinoid OMTs.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results support increased HDMBOA-Glc, depletion of DIMBOA-Glc/DIMBOA, reduced callose induction, improved caterpillar resistance in preference/feeding assays, and improved aphid performance on OeBx12 plants.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are in Results/Methods: transgenic overexpression, qRT-PCR, LC-based metabolite profiling in leaves/phloem, infiltration and callose assays, caterpillar assays, and aphid performance assays.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The study tests effects under S. littoralis feeding, S. avenae infestation, and chitosan elicitation.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results directly show increased caterpillar resistance but decreased aphid resistance and reduced callose inducibility; fig. S2 shows increased ferulic acid.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a reasonable implication from the results, but the paper does not explicitly propose this applied deployment statement in Results.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper uses heterologous maize ZmBx12 expression in wheat, and reports no clear effects on growth, sugars, starch, soluble protein, and no consistent amino acid changes.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are directly supported by the Results: metabolic conversion in leaves/phloem, suppression of chitosan- and aphid-induced callose, and opposite effects on caterpillar versus aphid resistance.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The paper lists the loci as 'Traes 4AL C467B516F' and 'Traes 2BL C467B516F'; the underscored accession formatting 'Traes_4AL_C467B516F; Traes_2BL_C467B516F' does not appear exactly.

Enzyme_Name.reason: TaBx10 is identified and characterized as a functional DIMBOA-Glc OMT in wheat.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: TaBx10 is studied within benzoxazinoid biosynthesis and defense regulation.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: In vitro, TaBX10 methylates DIMBOA-Glc and DIM2BOA-Glc, supporting this O-methylation branch description.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: The paper places this O-methylation step downstream of the conserved core benzoxazinoid pathway and as part of the defense switch.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Results explicitly state purified recombinant TaBX10 converted DIMBOA-Glc and DIM2BOA-Glc into HDMBOA-Glc and HDM2BOA-Glc, respectively, in the presence of S-adenosyl-l-methionine.

Primary_Substrate.reason: DIMBOA-Glc and DIM2BOA-Glc are the tested substrates for recombinant TaBX10.

Primary_Product.reason: HDMBOA-Glc and HDM2BOA-Glc are the reported reaction products.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: TaBX10 directly forms the target benzoxazinoid products in vitro.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper presents DIMBOA-Glc O-methylation generally as a regulatory switch, but it does not directly demonstrate TaBx10 itself is rate-limiting; contribution to herbivore-induced HDMBOA-Glc formation is inferred rather than proven in planta.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: HDMBOA-Glc is one direct product of TaBX10 activity and central metabolite linked to the switch.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper links HDMBOA-Glc with caterpillar deterrence/antixenosis and the defense switch away from DIMBOA-Glc-dependent aphid resistance, though this function is not shown by TaBx10 transgenics directly.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Herbivore-induced HDMBOA-Glc changes are measured in wheat leaves.

Applied_Species.reason: TaBx10 is a wheat gene studied from wheat leaves/cDNA.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The wheat species is stated as Triticum aestivum.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The study used the wheat line Bobwhite SH 98 26.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: TaBx10 cDNA was amplified from herbivore-damaged wheat leaves of the study genotype Bobwhite SH 98 26.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: RNA-seq and qRT-PCR confirmed strong up-regulation after S. littoralis herbivory.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The paper states TaBx10 expression was similarly induced by S. littoralis in wild-type and OeBx12 lines.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: TaBx10 is an O-methyltransferase; candidate genes were identified as OMTs with moderate similarity to ZmBx7.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The enzyme activity is shown in vitro and herbivore induction is shown, but the statement that it likely underlies herbivore-induced DIMBOA-Glc O-methylation in wheat is inferential; the discussion explicitly says in planta functional validation is still needed.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed approaches are described: RNA-seq candidate discovery, qRT-PCR, cloning from cDNA, heterologous E. coli expression, purified enzyme assay with LC-MS/MS, and phylogenetic analysis.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: TaBx10 induction was studied under Spodoptera littoralis feeding.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper establishes the trade-off for DIMBOA-Glc O-methylation generally, but not specifically by manipulating TaBx10 in planta.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a plausible application idea, but it is not explicitly stated or directly tested in the paper.

Omics_Data.reason: Transcriptome sequencing/RNA-seq was used, and raw reads were deposited under SRA accession SRP148745.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper states wheat lacks close orthologs of maize ZmBx10/11/12/14, TaBx10 is only distantly related, and discussion notes in planta functional validation remains needed.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are directly supported: transcriptomic identification of candidates and TaBx10 isolation, in vitro methylation of DIMBOA-Glc and DIM2BOA-Glc, and phylogenetic evidence for independent evolution from maize Bx10/11/12/14.

corrections.reason: The paper lists the loci as 'Traes 4AL C467B516F' and 'Traes 2BL C467B516F'; the underscored accession formatting 'Traes_4AL_C467B516F; Traes_2BL_C467B516F' does not appear exactly.
